description: Curated catalog of well-known remote MCP servers that speak OAuth 2.1 per the 2025-06-18 spec (or public no-auth for reference servers). Users get one-click add from the Connectors page. Every entry has a verified reachable URL — expected codes are 401 (auth required, server alive) or 405 (wrong HTTP verb for HEAD, server alive) for OAuth-protected servers, 200 for public servers. URLs may change; file is refreshed on every wikimem release.

verification_notes.401: Server alive and requires OAuth (expected for protected MCP endpoints — the client gets WWW-Authenticate: Bearer with resource_metadata pointer).
verification_notes.405: Server alive; HEAD not supported but POST /mcp works. Streamable HTTP transport uses POST only.
verification_notes.200: Public server, no auth needed — reference implementation or docs-query server.
verification_notes.410: SSE endpoint deprecated; use /mcp (Streamable HTTP) instead.
verification_notes.404: URL no longer valid — excluded from this catalog.

servers.name: WikiMem (self-test)
servers.description: Local WikiMem instance. Proves the client can OAuth against our own server end-to-end. Required entry — bootstrap target for every `pnpm test`.
servers.notes: Only shown when WIKIMEM_SHOW_SELF_TEST=1 or when running on localhost. WikiMem's own MCP server (src/mcp/) implements RFC 7591 Dynamic Client Registration; CIMD support is planned for v0.11.

servers.name: Slack
servers.description: Search Slack channels, post messages, manage canvases, query users. First-party Anthropic Claude Connector. OAuth 2.1 + PKCE S256. Pre-registered client (Slack Enterprise/Grid).
servers.notes: Pre-registered only: Slack does not publish a DCR endpoint — Anthropic's Claude has a client_id pre-registered with Slack. For self-hosted clients, register a Slack app at https://api.slack.com/apps with Bearer auth. PRM at https://mcp.slack.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource verified — full OAuth 2.1 metadata published.

servers.name: Google Workspace (Gmail + Drive + Calendar + Chat)
servers.description: Google Workspace remote MCP — Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Chat, Docs, Sheets, People. Official Google MCP. OAuth 2.0 — pre-registered client (Google Cloud Console). Multi-endpoint: this entry covers Gmail; sibling endpoints exist at drivemcp/calendarmcp/chatmcp/docsmcp/sheetsmcp.googleapis.com.
servers.notes: Pre-registered only: Google Workspace MCPs require a Google Cloud OAuth Client ID (Web application type for Claude — redirect URI: https://claude.ai/api/mcp/auth_callback). Sibling URLs (all 405 verified live): https://drivemcp.googleapis.com/mcp/v1, https://calendarmcp.googleapis.com/mcp/v1, https://chatmcp.googleapis.com/mcp/v1, https://docsmcp.googleapis.com/mcp/v1, https://sheetsmcp.googleapis.com/mcp/v1, https://people.googleapis.com/mcp/v1. WikiMem may surface separate cards per product in future v0.11+; for now this is the umbrella entry that gmail/gdrive/gcal/gchat alias to.

servers.name: Microsoft 365 (Outlook + OneDrive + Teams + SharePoint)
servers.description: Microsoft 365 MCP Server for Enterprise — Outlook Mail, Calendar, Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint via Microsoft Graph. Official Microsoft MCP. OAuth 2.0 (Microsoft Entra ID). Tenant-provisioned (admin must Grant-EntraBetaMCPServerPermission).
servers.notes: Pre-registered + tenant-provisioned: Microsoft Entra admin must run Grant-EntraBetaMCPServerPermission to register the MCP Server in the tenant before users can connect. Auth server is https://login.microsoftonline.com/organizations/v2.0. Scope format: api://e8c77dc2-69b3-43f4-bc51-3213c9d915b4/.default plus MCP.* delegated permissions. PRM at https://mcp.svc.cloud.microsoft/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/enterprise verified.

servers.name: GitLab
servers.description: GitLab MCP Server — query and modify GitLab issues, merge requests, pipelines, and code. Official GitLab MCP. OAuth 2.0 + Dynamic Client Registration. Premium/Ultimate tier required for gitlab.com; self-managed instances supported (replace host).
servers.notes: DCR-supported: GitLab MCP server supports OAuth 2.0 Dynamic Client Registration per GitLab 18.7 (MCP spec 2025-06-18 compliant). PRM at https://gitlab.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/api/v4/mcp verified. Self-hosted: replace https://gitlab.com with your instance URL. Sibling endpoint https://gitlab.com/api/v4/orbit/mcp also exists for GitLab Orbit.

servers.name: Dropbox
servers.description: Browse, search, read, and write Dropbox files. Official Dropbox MCP. OAuth 2.0 (Dropbox app authorization).
servers.notes: Pre-registered: Dropbox does not currently publish a DCR endpoint. Register a Dropbox app at https://www.dropbox.com/developers/apps and paste client_id into wikimem's advanced form. PRM at https://mcp.dropbox.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp verified — auth server is https://www.dropbox.com.

servers.name: Airtable
servers.description: Search, create, and update Airtable bases, tables, and records. Official Airtable MCP. OAuth 2.0 (with PAT fallback).
servers.notes: Released April 2026. Pre-registered: register an Airtable OAuth app at https://airtable.com/create/oauth and paste client_id into wikimem's advanced form. PAT auth also supported (Authorization: Bearer pat...). PRM at https://mcp.airtable.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource verified — auth server is https://airtable.com/oauth2/v1.

servers.name: Cloudflare API (Codemode)
servers.description: Codemode-style access to the entire Cloudflare API — 2,500+ endpoints across DNS, Workers, R2, Zero Trust. Official Cloudflare MCP. OAuth 2.1.
servers.notes: Top-level Cloudflare MCP — covers ALL Cloudflare API surface in one server. Use product-specific servers (cloudflare-radar, cloudflare-browser, etc.) for narrower scopes. PRM at https://mcp.cloudflare.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource verified.

servers.name: Cloudflare Radar
servers.description: Query Cloudflare Radar for global internet traffic insights, BGP, DNS, attack data, and traffic anomalies. Public web telemetry. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare Browser Rendering
servers.description: Render web pages, screenshot, scrape, and interact with browsers via Cloudflare's hosted Chromium. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare AI Gateway
servers.description: Query AI Gateway logs and analytics — see token usage, latency, and request patterns. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare Workers Builds
servers.description: Manage Cloudflare Workers builds — view build status, trigger rebuilds, inspect logs. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare Containers
servers.description: Manage Cloudflare Containers — deploy, monitor, and scale container workloads. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare DNS Analytics
servers.description: Query DNS analytics across Cloudflare zones — query volume, response codes, country breakdowns. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare Logpush
servers.description: Query Cloudflare HTTP, firewall, and Logpush logs across zones. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare AI Search (AutoRAG)
servers.description: Cloudflare AutoRAG — managed RAG over your indexed sources. Query, ingest, manage retrievers. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare Audit Logs
servers.description: Query Cloudflare account audit logs — who did what, when, from which IP. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare GraphQL Analytics
servers.description: Run Cloudflare GraphQL Analytics queries across all data sources. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare One CASB
servers.description: Query Cloudflare One CASB findings — SaaS security misconfigurations, sensitive data exposure. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare DEX
servers.description: Cloudflare Digital Experience Monitoring — synthetic tests, real user monitoring, app health. OAuth 2.1.

servers.name: Cloudflare Documentation
servers.description: Search and query Cloudflare's full product documentation. Public, no auth.
servers.notes: Public no-auth MCP. HEAD/POST without proper Accept negotiation returns 405/406; full Streamable HTTP POST with Accept: application/json works.

servers.name: Linear
servers.description: Read and write Linear issues, projects, and cycles. First-party Claude Connector. OAuth 2.1 + DCR.

servers.name: Notion
servers.description: Search and write Notion workspace pages, databases, and blocks. First-party Claude Connector. OAuth 2.1 + DCR.

servers.name: Atlassian (Jira + Confluence)
servers.description: Query Jira tickets and Confluence pages across your Atlassian cloud. First-party Claude Connector — uses pre-registered client (requires site URL during auth).
servers.notes: Pre-registered only: Atlassian does not publish a DCR endpoint — Claude's client_id is registered with Atlassian. For self-hosted clients, register at https://developer.atlassian.com/console/myapps/ and use your own client_id.

servers.name: Asana
servers.description: Query and manage Asana tasks, projects, and workspaces. First-party Claude Connector. OAuth 2.1 + DCR.

servers.name: Intercom
servers.description: Search conversations, contacts, and tickets in Intercom. First-party Claude Connector.

servers.name: Sentry
servers.description: Query errors, performance issues, and deploy monitoring from Sentry. Official Sentry MCP — full OAuth 2.1 + DCR via Cloudflare Workers-OAuth-Provider. Use /mcp (Streamable HTTP); /sse endpoint is deprecated.

servers.name: GitHub (Copilot)
servers.description: Browse repos, search code, read/write issues and PRs. Official GitHub remote MCP. OAuth 2.1 — pre-registered client (Copilot subscription required).
servers.notes: Pre-registered only: Anthropic's Claude has a client_id pre-registered with GitHub. For self-hosted clients, register an OAuth App at https://github.com/settings/developers and paste the client_id into wikimem's advanced connector form.

servers.name: Supabase
servers.description: Query Supabase Postgres database, run SQL, inspect schema, manage migrations. Official Supabase MCP. OAuth 2.1 + DCR.

servers.name: Vercel
servers.description: Inspect deployments, tail build logs, manage project config. Official Vercel MCP. OAuth 2.1 + DCR.

servers.name: Neon (Postgres)
servers.description: Serverless Postgres database management — run queries, branch databases, view logs. Official Neon MCP. OAuth 2.1 + DCR.

servers.name: Prisma Postgres
servers.description: Serverless Postgres with Prisma ORM integration. Run queries and inspect schema. Official Prisma MCP.

servers.name: Cloudflare Workers Bindings
servers.description: Manage Cloudflare Workers, KV, R2, D1, and Durable Objects via MCP. Official Cloudflare MCP.

servers.name: Cloudflare Observability
servers.description: Query Workers logs and analytics. Useful for debugging your own deployed Workers. OAuth against Cloudflare account.

servers.name: Netlify
servers.description: Manage Netlify sites, deploys, and environment variables. Official Netlify MCP. OAuth 2.1 + DCR.

servers.name: Stripe
servers.description: Query payments, customers, subscriptions, invoices. Official Stripe MCP — restricted-key (API key) based OR OAuth 2.1 depending on account setup.
servers.notes: Supports both API key (restricted keys) and OAuth 2.1. For WikiMem MCP client, use OAuth path.

servers.name: PayPal
servers.description: PayPal transactions, invoicing, and subscription management. Official PayPal MCP. OAuth via PayPal Developer.

servers.name: Square
servers.description: Query Square payments, orders, and inventory. Official Square MCP.

servers.name: Plaid
servers.description: Connect to bank accounts, view transactions via Plaid Dashboard. Official Plaid MCP — pre-registered client required.
servers.notes: Pre-registered only: Plaid does not support DCR. Register at https://dashboard.plaid.com/ and paste client_id into wikimem's advanced form.

servers.name: Canva
servers.description: Create and manage Canva designs from MCP clients. Official Canva MCP. OAuth 2.1 + DCR.

servers.name: Webflow
servers.description: Read and publish Webflow CMS items. Official Webflow MCP.

servers.name: Wix
servers.description: Manage Wix sites, CMS collections, and e-commerce. Official Wix MCP.

servers.name: monday.com
servers.description: Query and update monday.com boards, items, and columns. Official monday.com MCP.

servers.name: Attio (CRM)
servers.description: Modern CRM — read and write companies, people, deals, and notes. Official Attio MCP. OAuth 2.1 + DCR.

servers.name: Close CRM
servers.description: Close CRM — read leads, contacts, opportunities, and call logs. Official Close MCP. Pre-registered only.
servers.notes: Pre-registered only: obtain a client_id from Close's developer dashboard.

servers.name: HubSpot
servers.description: HubSpot CRM — contacts, deals, companies, and marketing. Official HubSpot MCP. Uses API key (HubSpot Access Token).
servers.notes: API key auth only (not OAuth): generate a Private App access token in HubSpot and pass via Authorization: Bearer header.

servers.name: Box
servers.description: Browse, search, and read Box files and folders. Official Box MCP. Pre-registered only (Box developer console).
servers.notes: Pre-registered only: create a Box Custom App, note the client_id/secret.

servers.name: Cloudinary (Assets)
servers.description: Manage Cloudinary media assets — upload, transform, search. Official Cloudinary MCP.

servers.name: Hugging Face
servers.description: Search models, datasets, Spaces, and papers on the Hugging Face Hub. Official HF MCP. Optional OAuth via ?login query param; otherwise public read access with HF_TOKEN header.
servers.notes: Use https://huggingface.co/mcp?login to trigger OAuth flow for authenticated access; otherwise works read-only without auth.

servers.name: DeepWiki (GitHub repo Q&A)
servers.description: Query any public GitHub repository as a wiki — get architectural summaries, code Q&A, and doc search. Public no-auth for public repos; API key for private.

servers.name: Context7 (Up-to-date library docs)
servers.description: Real-time library documentation for React, Next.js, Prisma, and 1000+ libraries. Upstash-hosted. API key header (CONTEXT7_API_KEY); /mcp/oauth endpoint available for OAuth.
servers.notes: For OAuth flow, use https://mcp.context7.com/mcp/oauth instead.

servers.name: Stytch (Auth)
servers.description: Manage Stytch users, sessions, and OAuth connections. Official Stytch MCP. OAuth 2.1 + DCR.

servers.name: Buildkite CI
servers.description: Query CI pipelines, builds, and agents. Official Buildkite MCP.

servers.name: Globalping (network diagnostics)
servers.description: Run pings, traceroutes, DNS, and HTTP checks from 500+ global locations. Official jsdelivr Globalping MCP.

servers.name: Zapier (8000+ apps)
servers.description: Controlled access to 8,000+ apps via Zapier's automation layer. Uses per-user API key.
servers.notes: API key (not OAuth): generate via Zapier MCP dashboard, pass via Authorization: Bearer header. Scopes selected at key creation time.

servers.name: Apify (Web scraping)
servers.description: Run 3000+ web scraping Actors on Apify. API key-based auth.

servers.name: AWS Knowledge Base
servers.description: Search AWS documentation, API references, and best practices. Official AWS MCP — no auth required for docs.

servers.name: Exa Search
servers.description: Neural web search optimized for AI agents — semantic, code-aware, and documentation-aware queries. Public with API key.

servers.name: Tavily (Web search for agents)
servers.description: Agent-optimized web search with filtering and clean content extraction. API key-based.

servers.name: Stack Overflow
servers.description: Search Stack Overflow and Stack Exchange questions and answers. Official Stack Overflow MCP.

servers.name: Turkish Airlines
servers.description: Check flight status, schedules, and book Turkish Airlines flights. Official THY MCP.

servers.name: Port (Internal Developer Portal)
servers.description: Query your Port IDP — services, entities, scorecards, and actions. Official Port MCP.

servers.name: Jam (Bug replays)
servers.description: Query Jam bug replays — console logs, network requests, and user sessions. Official Jam MCP.

servers.name: Fireflies (Meeting notes)
servers.description: Search meeting transcripts, summaries, and action items from Fireflies.ai. Official Fireflies MCP.

servers.name: Egnyte (Document management)
servers.description: Enterprise file management — search, read, and share Egnyte documents. Official Egnyte MCP.

servers.name: Instant (InstantDB)
servers.description: Query InstantDB — real-time reactive database for apps. Official Instant MCP.

servers.name: GitMCP (any GitHub repo as MCP)
servers.description: Turn any GitHub repository into an MCP server — great for querying project READMEs, code, and issues. Public, no auth.
servers.notes: Template URL: https://gitmcp.io/{owner}/{repo} — replace the path to query any public repo.

servers.name: Todoist
servers.description: Official Todoist remote MCP server (Doist). Manage tasks, projects, labels, comments via natural language. OAuth 2.1 with PRM at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp.
servers.notes: PRM verified: resource=https://ai.todoist.net/mcp, authorization_servers=[https://todoist.com], bearer_methods_supported=[header], scopes_supported=[data:read_write]. Returns 401 with WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata=<PRM-URL> per RFC 9728. Maintained by Doist (Doist/todoist-ai repo). Pre-registered client model — register a Todoist app to obtain client_id.

servers.name: OpenAI Developer Docs
servers.description: Official OpenAI Developers documentation MCP — read-only search and page fetching over OpenAI docs. Public no-auth server. NOT the OpenAI API itself; documentation queries only.
servers.notes: Verified: initialize call returns valid MCP response with serverInfo.name=openai-docs-mcp, protocolVersion=2025-06-18, capabilities.tools.listChanged=true. Streamable HTTP transport, hosted on Vercel. Public — no OAuth required. Documentation-only; does NOT call OpenAI API on user behalf.

preregistered_only_setup.description: Some MCP servers above (GitHub, Atlassian, Box, Close, Plaid, Slack, Google Workspace, Microsoft Graph, Dropbox, Airtable) do not support RFC 7591 DCR. To use them with WikiMem's MCP client, follow these steps per provider:

github-copilot.notes: Create a new OAuth App. Copy client_id and client_secret. Paste into WikiMem Connectors → 'Add MCP Server (Advanced)'.

atlassian.notes: Create an OAuth 2.0 (3LO) app. Enable the Jira and Confluence scopes. Copy client_id/secret.

box.notes: Create a Custom App with OAuth 2.0 user auth. Enable required scopes. Copy client_id/secret.

close-crm.notes: Create an OAuth app in Close's developer settings.

plaid.notes: Register redirect URI in Plaid Dashboard. Copy client_id/secret.

slack.notes: Create a new Slack app, set OAuth & Permissions → Redirect URLs, copy client_id/secret. Slack MCP requires Slack Enterprise/Grid. Anthropic has its own client_id pre-registered for claude.ai users — self-hosted clients need their own.

google-workspace.notes: In Google Cloud Console, create OAuth 2.0 credentials (type: Web application for Claude/wikimem, Desktop application for Gemini CLI). Add redirect URI. Enable required Google Workspace APIs (Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Chat). Copy client_id/secret.

microsoft-graph.notes: Microsoft Entra admin must FIRST run Grant-EntraBetaMCPServerPermission -ApplicationName VisualStudioCode (or your app) to provision the MCP Server in the tenant. Then register app in Entra admin center, add redirect URI, request delegated MCP.* scopes, and grant admin consent. Single-tenant only.

dropbox.notes: Create a Dropbox app, choose 'Scoped access', select required permissions (account_info.read, files.metadata.read, files.content.read, files.content.write). Add redirect URI. Copy app key (client_id) and app secret.

airtable.notes: Create an Airtable OAuth integration, set redirect URI, select scopes. Airtable also supports PAT auth as a fallback (Authorization: Bearer pat... in header).
